name: Kannada
description: Kannada is a Southern Dravidian language and the official language of the Indian state of Karnataka. It has a rich literary heritage and is one of the oldest Dravidian languages with a written tradition. The Kannada script is closely related to the Telugu script, both deriving from the Kadamba script.
historicalNotes: Kannada has one of the oldest Dravidian literary traditions, with the Halmidi inscription (450 CE) being the earliest known Kannada-language stone inscription. The Kavirajamarga (850 CE) is the earliest extant work on Kannada grammar and poetics. Kannada was designated a classical language of India in 2008. The language has produced notable literary figures, including eight Jnanpith Award winners — more than any other Dravidian language.
